blurb: Plans, designs, builds, and manages Alberta's public infrastructure including schools, health care facilities, and government buildings — delivering capital projects, managing government-owned properties, and ensuring procurement accountability.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Alberta has the schools, health facilities, and public buildings communities need
goals.description: The ministry plans, designs, and delivers major capital projects including new schools, health care facilities, government buildings, and community infrastructure to serve Alberta's growing and changing population.

foundational_services.name: Develop the provincial capital plan and prioritize infrastructure investments
foundational_services.desc: Coordinates the provincial capital planning process, assesses infrastructure needs across ministries, prioritizes projects, and allocates capital funding for schools, health facilities, government buildings, and other public infrastructure to meet population growth and service demands.
foundational_services.users: Government planners, Treasury Board, school boards, health authorities

main_services.name: Track a school or health facility capital project

supporting.name: Check the status of a new school construction or modernization project
supporting.desc: School boards, parents, and communities can track the status of approved school capital projects including new construction, modernizations, and replacements. Project information includes location, scope, timeline, and current construction phase.
supporting.users: Parents, school boards, community members in growing areas

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of parents and community members track school projects
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta typically has dozens of school projects underway at any time

economic_impact.note: School construction directly enables education access for growing communities

supporting.name: Check the status of a health facility construction or expansion project
supporting.desc: Communities can track the status of health facility capital projects including new hospitals, expansions, and major renovations. Project details include scope, timeline, construction phase, and expected completion.
supporting.users: Community members, health administrators, municipal councils

reach_estimate.estimate: Dozens of health facility projects affecting hundreds of communities
reach_estimate.basis: Major health capital investments include hospitals, cancer centres, and patient bed expansions

economic_impact.note: Health facility investment directly expands health care capacity and patient access

main_services.name: Bid on or participate in a public infrastructure project

supporting.name: Find and bid on government infrastructure tenders
supporting.desc: Construction firms, architects, engineers, and other contractors can find open tenders for government infrastructure projects including schools, health facilities, government buildings, and maintenance contracts. Bidding follows the Public Works Act procurement framework.
supporting.users: Construction companies, engineering firms, architects, subcontractors

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of firms participate in government infrastructure tendering
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta's capital plan represents billions of dollars in annual construction activity

economic_impact.note: Open tendering supports fair competition and maximizes value for public infrastructure spending

supporting.name: Resolve a payment dispute on a public works contract
supporting.desc: Contractors and subcontractors on public works projects can use the prompt payment and adjudication framework under the Public Works Act to resolve payment disputes. The Act sets mandatory payment timelines and provides binding adjudication for disputed invoices.
supporting.users: General contractors, subcontractors, suppliers on government projects

reach_estimate.basis: Prompt payment provisions apply to all public works contracts

economic_impact.note: Prompt payment protections support contractor cash flow and keep projects on schedule

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Government facilities are well-managed and serve Albertans effectively
goals.description: The ministry manages the Government of Alberta's portfolio of owned and leased buildings, provides accommodation for government offices, maintains facilities to safety and functionality standards, and disposes of surplus properties.

foundational_services.name: Manage the government facility portfolio and accommodation standards
foundational_services.desc: Administers the government's building portfolio including space planning, facility condition assessments, maintenance programs, energy management, and accommodation standards for government offices across the province.
foundational_services.users: Government accommodation planners, facility management staff

main_services.name: Access government property and leasing services

supporting.name: Buy surplus government property
supporting.desc: When government properties (land or buildings) are no longer needed, they are made available for sale. Interested buyers can find listings of available surplus government properties across the province.
supporting.users: Developers, investors, municipalities, members of the public

reach_estimate.estimate: Dozens of surplus properties listed annually
reach_estimate.basis: Ongoing portfolio optimization releases surplus assets

economic_impact.note: Surplus property sales return capital to the public and enable community development

supporting.name: Lease space to or from the Government of Alberta
supporting.desc: Property owners can offer space for government lease, and government agencies can request accommodation through the ministry's realty services. The ministry manages hundreds of leases across the province for government offices and service delivery locations.
supporting.users: Commercial property owners, landlords, government accommodation requesters

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of active leases managed province-wide
reach_estimate.basis: Government leases office and service delivery space across Alberta

economic_impact.note: Efficient accommodation supports government service delivery and sustains commercial real estate

main_services.name: Report a facility issue or access building services

supporting.name: Report a maintenance or safety issue at a government building
supporting.desc: Tenants, staff, and members of the public can report maintenance issues, safety concerns, or facility problems at government-owned buildings. The ministry manages building systems, safety compliance, and capital maintenance across the government facility portfolio.
supporting.users: Government staff in provincial buildings, members of the public visiting government offices

reach_estimate.basis: Large portfolio of government buildings requiring ongoing maintenance

economic_impact.note: Responsive maintenance protects public assets and ensures safe, functional workplaces

supporting.name: Access technical standards and guidelines for public buildings
supporting.desc: Architects, engineers, and project managers working on government infrastructure projects can access the ministry's technical standards and design guidelines for public buildings, covering accessibility, energy performance, building systems, and safety requirements.
supporting.users: Design professionals, project managers, construction firms working on government projects

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of design professionals reference government standards
reach_estimate.basis: All government capital projects must comply with provincial technical standards

economic_impact.note: Consistent standards ensure quality, accessibility, and long-term durability of public buildings

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Public infrastructure is financed and delivered with accountability and value
goals.description: The ministry ensures public infrastructure is delivered with fiscal accountability through transparent procurement, alternative financing models including public-private partnerships, heritage protection during development, and regular reporting on capital project performance.

foundational_services.name: Develop procurement policy and alternative financing frameworks
foundational_services.desc: Develops procurement standards, prompt payment policies, public-private partnership frameworks, and alternative financing models to deliver infrastructure projects efficiently while maximizing value for Albertans.
foundational_services.users: Government procurement staff, P3 partners, Treasury Board

main_services.name: Engage with public-private partnership and alternative financing opportunities

supporting.name: Learn about public-private partnership opportunities for infrastructure
supporting.desc: Private sector firms and investors can learn about Alberta's public-private partnership (P3) model, current and upcoming P3 opportunities, and the framework for partnering with government to deliver public infrastructure such as schools, roads, and health facilities.
supporting.users: Construction firms, investors, financial institutions, infrastructure developers

reach_estimate.estimate: Dozens of firms actively engaged in P3 opportunities
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta uses P3 for select major infrastructure projects

economic_impact.note: P3 models can accelerate infrastructure delivery and transfer construction and maintenance risk

supporting.name: Review capital plan reports and infrastructure spending accountability
supporting.desc: Albertans can review the provincial capital plan, annual reports on infrastructure spending, project performance, and financial accountability for public infrastructure investments. Reports are published through the ministry and budget documents.

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of residents and stakeholders access capital plan information
reach_estimate.basis: The capital plan represents billions in public spending with broad public interest

economic_impact.note: Transparent reporting builds public confidence in infrastructure spending decisions

main_services.name: Protect heritage resources during infrastructure development

supporting.name: Request a heritage resource impact assessment for a project
supporting.desc: When an infrastructure or development project may affect designated historical resources, heritage buildings, or archaeological sites, proponents must obtain a heritage resource impact assessment. The ministry coordinates with heritage authorities to protect significant sites.
supporting.users: Project proponents, developers, construction firms near heritage sites

reach_estimate.basis: All major projects in areas with potential heritage resources require assessment

economic_impact.note: Heritage protection preserves Alberta's history while enabling responsible development

supporting.name: Look up designated heritage buildings and provincial historic sites
supporting.desc: Albertans, researchers, and developers can look up buildings and sites designated as provincial historic resources. Designated resources have legal protection and specific requirements for any proposed alterations or nearby development.
supporting.users: Property owners, developers, historians, researchers, tourists

reach_estimate.basis: Alberta has hundreds of designated historic resources with growing public and development interest

economic_impact.note: Heritage registry information supports due diligence for development and promotes cultural tourism
